tools.name: openmandate_create_mandate
tools.description:
Create a new mandate on OpenMandate. Provide what the user is looking for (want) and what they bring to the table (offer). The user's verified contacts are automatically attached.

Returns the mandate with follow-up questions. You MUST relay these questions to the user and collect their actual answe

tools.name: openmandate_submit_answers
tools.description:
Submit the user's answers to pending intake questions. The mandate must be in 'intake' status with pending questions.

IMPORTANT: Before calling this, you must show each pending question to the user and collect their real answer. Never fabricate or infer answers. OpenMandate may return additional qu

tools.name: openmandate_get_mandate
tools.description: Get a mandate by ID. Returns the mandate's current status, any pending intake questions, and summary once active. Use this to check progress or retrieve questions after creating a mandate.

tools.name: openmandate_list_mandates
tools.description: List the user's open mandates. Returns non-closed mandates by default. Pass status to filter (e.g. status='closed' for history).

tools.name: openmandate_close_mandate
tools.description: Close (withdraw) a mandate. This is permanent — the mandate will stop matching and cannot be reopened. Only close if the user explicitly wants to withdraw.

tools.name: openmandate_list_matches
tools.description: List matches for the authenticated user. Returns matches with compatibility grade, strengths, and concerns. Check this periodically after a mandate becomes active.

tools.name: openmandate_get_match
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific match, including compatibility grade, strengths, concerns, and — if both parties accepted — the counterparty's contact information.

tools.name: openmandate_respond_to_match
tools.description: Accept or decline a match. If you accept and the other party also accepts, contact info is revealed to both sides. Declining is permanent for this match.

tools.name: openmandate_submit_outcome
tools.description:
Report how a confirmed match went. Available after both parties accepted and contact info was revealed.

Outcomes:
- 'succeeded': Found the right person. Mandate closes.
- 'failed': Didn't work out. Mandate reactivates for new matches.
- 'ongoing': Still in conversation. OpenMandate checks back late

tools.name: openmandate_list_contacts
tools.description: List the user's verified contacts. These are the contacts revealed to the other party on match confirmation. Each contact has an ID, type, display label, and verification status.

tools.name: openmandate_add_contact
tools.description:
Add a new contact for the user. A verification code (OTP) will be sent to the contact address. The user must verify the contact using openmandate_verify_contact before it can be used on mandates.

The first contact added becomes the primary contact automatically.

tools.name: openmandate_verify_contact
tools.description: Verify a contact by submitting the OTP code sent to it. The user must check their email for the 8-digit code. After verification, the contact can be used on mandates.

tools.name: openmandate_update_contact
tools.description: Update a contact's display label or set it as the primary contact. Setting a contact as primary will unset any other primary contact.

tools.name: openmandate_delete_contact
tools.description: Delete a contact. If the deleted contact was primary, the next verified contact is automatically promoted. This is permanent.

tools.name: openmandate_resend_otp
tools.description: Resend the verification code for a pending contact. Use this if the user didn't receive the code or it expired (codes expire after 10 minutes). Rate limited to 3 per contact per hour.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "openmandate_create_mandate" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Create a new mandate on OpenMandate. Provide what the user is looking for (want) and what they bring to the table (offer). The user's verified contacts are auto…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

evidence.snippet: Submit the user's answers to pending intake questions. The mandate must be in 'intake' status with pending questions. IMPORTANT: Before calling this, you must s…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "openmandate_submit_answers" stacks urgency/override words (important, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Submit the user's answers to pending intake questions. The mandate must be in 'intake' status with pending questions. IMPORTANT: Before calling this, you must s…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "openmandate_submit_outcome" stacks urgency/override words (important, always). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Report how a confirmed match went. Available after both parties accepted and contact info was revealed. Outcomes: - 'succeeded': Found the right person. Mandate…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: openmandate_verify_contact · Private data: openmandate_create_mandate, openmandate_get_match, openmandate_respond_to_match, openmandate_submit_outcome, openmandate_list_contacts, openmandate_add_contact, openmandate_verify_contact, openmandate_update_contact, openmandate_delete_contact, openmandate_resend_otp · Public sink: openmandate_submit_answers, openmandate_submit_outcome, openmandate_verify_contact · Destructive: openmandate_delete_contact.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: openmandate_verify_contact · Private data: openmandate_create_mandate, openmandate_get_match, openmandate_respond_to_match, openmandate_submit_outcome, openmandate_list_contacts, openmandate_add_contact, openmandate_verify_contact, openmandate_update_contact, openmandate_delete_contact, openmandate_resend_otp · Public sink: openmandate_submit_answers, openmandate_submit_outcome, openmandate_verify_contact · Destructive: openmandate_delete_contact

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
